blocks.text: Turn up the floor carpet and insulator cutout (A) to make the floor a steel plate surface. Pedal height is measured from the projection of the floorboard (B) to the top of the pedal center. Start the engine. Gap with floorboards when stepped on:. After depressing the brake pedal 2-3 times, measure the clearance (C) between the brake pedal and the projection of the floor plate when the brake pedal is depressed. Clearance to floor plate (at tread force of 196 N (20 kgf)) 144mm or more Pedal height when not depressed:. Measure the height (D) from the projection of the floor plate to the top center of the brake pedal without the brake pedal depressed. Height: 1.5 201±5mm
With the engine stopped, depress the brake pedal 2-3 times. Measure the amount of travel (play) (A) until resistance is felt by pressing the brake pedal with a finger. Play:. 1－6mm
When making adjustments to increase pedal height, remove the switch to prevent damage to the brake switch.
Loosen the yoke locknut (A) on the push rod. Turn the push rod (B) to adjust the pedal height. Tighten the yoke lock nut.

With the pedal pulled all the way forward, push the switch in until the threaded end (B) of the brake switch (A) touches the pedal stop bracket (C). Turn the brake switch clockwise 45° to lock. The clearance between the pedal stop brackets is automatically adjusted to 0.7 mm when the brake switch is locked.

items.text: After adjustment, check that there is pedal play.
After adjustment, check that there is pedal play.
If there is no play, brake drag may occur, so be sure to inspect.
After adjustment, check that the brake light is working properly.
After adjustment, check that the brake light is working properly.
